original_description: Capture knowledge from the current session on demand — decisions, conventions, failures, observations. User-invocable complement to the automatic close-loop extraction by record-extractor. Use when you want to explicitly document learnings mid-session without waiting for pipeline completion.

train_results.query: compound - save the pattern we settled on for the event sourcing aggregate roots
note down that auth0 tenant IDs are case-sensitive - wasted 2hrs on this
save what we learned about the retry logic before I close this session
I want to preserve the findings from this debugging session before we move on
log that we tried redis for rate limiting but it caused thundering herd on cold start
document the decision we made to use optimistic locking instead of pessimistic
generate a changelog from the commits we made this sprint
I want to run /dream to consolidate all the knowledge from the last few sessions
document the architecture of the event pipeline in the codebase diagnostics output
at end of session extract all decisions and patterns we established today
we discovered the cache invalidation bug - create a seeds issue so we don't forget it
write a HANDOFF.md so the next session can pick up from where we are now

test_results.query: mid-session knowledge dump - we just figured out why the migrations were failing
record that the webhook signature validation has to come before body parsing or it breaks
capture this convention - all handlers must return Result types, not throw
explicitly document learnings from this spike before we switch contexts
brainstorm the best way to capture and persist knowledge across sessions in our platform
check the handoff doc from yesterday and resume where we left off on the auth refactor
write tests to document what the legacy payment processor actually does before I refactor it
run the close-loop pipeline and record everything from this session to mulch
